hero.badge: Premier Institute for Humanities & Social Sciences
hero.description: Career Point University offers cutting-edge programs shaping future civil servants, researchers, writers, and social leaders.

vision.title: Vision
vision.label: Our Purpose
vision.text: To be a center of excellence in humanities education, nurturing creative minds that contribute meaningfully to society and culture at national and global levels.

mission.title: Mission
mission.label: Our Goal
points: Promote interdisciplinary research in arts, literature, and social sciences.
Encourage creative expression, critical analysis, and open debates.
Preserve and promote cultural heritage through academic practices.
Nurture scholars and critical thinkers through modern research methodologies.
Prepare students for diverse careers in media, education, and public service.

programmes.subtitle: Academic Programmes
programmes.description: Explore our diverse range of undergraduate, postgraduate, and doctoral programs designed to foster deep analytical and social skills.

ba.title: Bachelor of Arts (B.A.)
ba.eligibility: 10+2 with minimum 50% marks from a recognized board
ba.description: The B.A. program focuses on preparing students for roles pertaining to civil services, journalism, social work, and the broader humanities sector.
ba.metaTitle: Bachelor of Arts (B.A.) | School of Arts & Humanities
ba.metaDescription: Pursue a 3-year Bachelor of Arts (B.A.) degree at Career Point University. Comprehensive foundation in English, History, Political Science, and Sociology.

hero.description: Comprehensive foundation in humanities with extensive focus on critical thinking, literature, historical analysis, governance, and social behavior.

overview.subtitle: What is the B.A. Programme?
paragraphs: The B.A. Programme is a three-year, six-semester journey that offers holistic knowledge of human culture, society, and governance. It fosters creativity, analytical thinking, and effective communication, addressing complex societal challenges and providing real-world perspectives.

Overall, the B.A. programme enables students with a comprehensive understanding of liberal arts principles, practical skills in writing and research, and opportunities for career growth in the dynamic and evolving fields of media, public administration, and education.

scope.body: The B.A. program is a comprehensive three-year undertaking that produces students with a solid foundation in humanities. The curriculum encompasses a wide range of subjects, spanning English Literature, Ancient and Modern History, Political Science, and Sociology. Additionally, students learn to leverage critical thinking, writing skills, and socio-economic analysis. Graduates can pursue roles as journalists, content strategists, civil servants, policy analysts, educators, and public relations officers.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in the world of humanities

curriculum.subtitle: A well-structured curriculum designed for excellence
curriculum.introNote: The B.A. programme is a comprehensive curriculum providing students with a solid foundation in arts, spanning subjects like English, History, Political Science, and Sociology, along with skill enhancement modules.
curriculum.outroNote: Graduates of B.A. are well prepared for diverse career specializations in media, public administration, NGOs, and the education sector.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure
accordionSections.content:
The Bachelor of Arts (B.A.) program at Career Point University is a comprehensive three-year undergraduate course designed to provide students with a solid foundation in humanities. The curriculum encompasses a wide range of subjects, including History, Political Science, Sociology, and English. In addition to these core topics, the program offers specialized modules in emerging fields like public policy, digital media, and social psychology, ensuring that students are well-versed in current societal trends.

The following table outlines the comprehensive course structure of the B.A. program. It is organized into core, elective, interdisciplinary, and skill enhancement components. Each category specifies the type of courses, their descriptions, and the corresponding credits, ensuring a well-rounded academic experience:

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Subjects covered in Semester 1 include: Introduction to English Literature, History of Ancient India, Understanding Political Theory, Environmental Studies, and Communication Skills. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Subjects covered in Semester 2 include: British Poetry & Drama, History of Medieval India, Political Process in India, Creative Writing, and Sociology Basics. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Subjects covered in Semester 3 include: American Literature, History of Modern India, Comparative Government and Politics, Skill Enhancement Course I. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Subjects covered in Semester 4 include: Women's Writing, History of Europe, Global Politics, Research Methodology, and Skill Enhancement Course II. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-5
accordionSections.content: Subjects covered in Semester 5 include: Postcolonial Literatures, History of Modern World, Public Administration, Discipline Specific Elective I, and Internship. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-6
accordionSections.content: Subjects covered in Semester 6 include: Modern European Drama, Issues in 20th Century World History, Indian Political Thought, Major Project, and Discipline Specific Elective II. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: University Core
accordionSections.content: University Core courses include: Environmental Science, Human Values & Professional Ethics, Indian Constitution & Traditional Knowledge, and Sports/NSS/NCC activities.

accordionSections.title: Departmental Elective
accordionSections.content: Departmental Elective options include: International Relations, Public Policy Analysis, Digital Humanities, Advanced Sociology, and Creative Non-Fiction.

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the B.A. programme

items.a: Candidates must have passed 10+2 (or equivalent) from a recognized board with a minimum of 50% marks in aggregate. Science/Commerce/Arts stream students are all eligible.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for B.A. admission?

items.a: B.A. is a 3-year (6 semester) programme. The tuition fee is ₹15,000 per semester. Additional charges include examination fee (₹2,000), development fee (₹2,000), and a one-time admission fee of ₹5,000.
items.q: What is the duration and fee structure of the B.A. programme?

items.a: Students can choose major subjects in English Literature, History, Political Science, and Sociology. These are offered as Discipline Specific Core and Electives.
items.q: What specializations are available in B.A. at Career Point University?

items.a: B.A. graduates frequently enter civil services, journalism, teaching, public relations, and NGO management. We also provide placement assistance with top media houses and NGOs.
items.q: What kind of career options can I expect after completing B.A.?

items.a: Yes. Merit-based scholarships of up to 100% on tuition fees are available for students scoring above 90% in their 12th board exams.
items.q: Is there any scholarship available for B.A. students?

items.a: Yes, our curriculum for History and Political Science is specifically designed to align with the syllabi of UPSC and other state-level civil service examinations.
items.q: Does the course help in UPSC/Civil Services preparation?

items.a: B.A. students have access to the Central Digital Library, Language and Communication Labs, and historical archives. Seminar halls for debates and mock parliaments are also available.
items.q: What facilities are available for B.A. students?

items.a: Yes. B.A. graduates can pursue M.A. in their respective subjects, B.Ed. for teaching, MBA, MSW, or degree in Law (L.L.B).
items.q: Can I pursue higher studies after completing B.A.?

b-lib.title: Bachelor of Library Science (B.Lib.)
b-lib.eligibility: Graduation in any discipline with minimum 50% marks
b-lib.description: A professional one-year program designed to train students in modern library management, digital informatics, and cataloging.
b-lib.metaTitle: Bachelor of Library Science (B.Lib.) | Career Point University
b-lib.metaDescription: Pursue a 1-Year B.Lib. degree at Career Point University. Master digital archiving, library software, and information retrieval.

hero.description: Bridge traditional library science with modern digital informatics. Master knowledge management, digital archiving, and modern cataloging systems.

overview.subtitle: What is the B.Lib. Programme?
paragraphs: The B.Lib. Programme is a highly specialized one-year, two-semester course that prepares students for professional roles in library and information centers. In the modern era of big data, librarians act as the crucial link between vast information resources and the users.
The curriculum is a perfect blend of traditional cataloging practices and modern IT applications like digital libraries, institutional repositories, and automated circulation systems.

sectionTitle.main: Scope of Bachelor of Library Science

scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in Knowledge Management
scope.body: The scope of B.Lib. goes far beyond traditional bookshelves. Graduates are in high demand across universities, schools (KVS, NVS), government archives, corporate law firms, and media houses. Roles include Assistant Librarian, Information Architect, Digital Archivist, Metadata Analyst, and Documentation Officer. With the digital boom, the need to organize and retrieve electronic information has made this profession highly lucrative.

curriculum.subtitle: A well-structured curriculum designed for excellence
curriculum.introNote: The B.Lib. programme provides intensive theoretical and practical training in Library and Information Science (LIS), integrating modern ICT tools.
curriculum.outroNote: Graduates are fully prepared to take up professional roles or advance to the Master of Library Science (M.Lib.) programme.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The Bachelor of Library Science (B.Lib.) program at Career Point University is an intensive one-year course. It bridges traditional library science with modern informatics. The course is divided equally into theoretical frameworks and hands-on laboratory practice with library automation software.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Subjects covered in Semester 1 include: Foundations of Library and Information Science, Library Classification (Theory), Library Cataloging (Theory), and Basics of Information Technology. Total Credits: 20

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Subjects covered in Semester 2 include: Library Management, Information Sources & Services, Library Classification (Practice), Library Cataloging (Practice), and Library Automation (IT Practical). Total Credits: 20

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the B.Lib. programme

items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for B.Lib. admission?
items.a: Candidates must have completed their Graduation in any stream (B.A, B.Sc, B.Com, etc.) from a recognized university with a minimum of 50% marks.

items.q: What is the duration of the course?
items.a: B.Lib. is a 1-year professional course divided into 2 semesters.

items.q: Are there government jobs after B.Lib.?
items.a: Yes, completing B.Lib. makes you eligible for Librarian and Assistant Librarian posts in government schools (KVS, NVS), colleges, public libraries, and research institutes like DRDO.

items.q: Does the course teach software or is it just theory?
items.a: It is highly tech-driven. You will have dedicated IT practicals where you will learn to use Library Automation Software (like KOHA, SOUL) and digital repository tools.

items.q: Can I pursue a Master's degree after this?
items.a: Yes, completing B.Lib. is the prerequisite to enroll in the Master of Library and Information Science (M.Lib.) programme.

roadmap.subtitle: Your intensive journey to becoming an Information Architect.

years.contentDesc: Master the theoretical foundations of classification, cataloging, and the societal role of libraries.
Apply theory to software. Learn how to manage a modern digital library infrastructure.

ma.title: Master of Arts (M.A.)
ma.eligibility: Graduation in relevant field with minimum 50% marks
ma.description: An advanced postgraduate program designed to develop subject matter expertise, critical research skills, and profound academic inquiry.
ma.metaTitle: Master of Arts (M.A.) | School of Arts & Humanities
ma.metaDescription: Pursue a 2-year Master of Arts (M.A.) degree at Career Point University. Specialize in English, History, or Political Science with a focus on advanced research.

hero.description: Deepen your expertise, engage in critical research, and prepare for prestigious roles in academia, civil services, and global policy leadership.

overview.subtitle: What is the M.A. Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Arts (M.A.) is a rigorous two-year, four-semester programme that transforms students into subject matter experts. It moves beyond textbook learning to focus on critical theory, independent research, and advanced analytical thinking.

Whether you are aiming to crack the UGC-NET/JRF for an academic career, preparing for higher civil services, or looking to enter the world of publishing and policy-making, the M.A. programme provides the intellectual depth required to succeed.

scope.body: An M.A. degree signifies a high level of academic rigor and intellectual maturity. Graduates are highly sought after as Assistant Professors, Senior Content Strategists, Policy Analysts, Museum Curators, and Political Consultants. Furthermore, the specialized knowledge gained during this programme acts as a massive catalyst for students appearing in the UPSC Civil Services Main Examination (Optional Subjects).
scope.subtitle: Elevating your academic and professional stature

curriculum.subtitle: A master-level curriculum focusing on research and theory
curriculum.introNote: The M.A. programme is characterized by advanced seminars, intensive reading lists, and a mandatory research dissertation.
curriculum.outroNote: Graduates leave not just with a degree, but with published research and specialized expertise in their chosen domain.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The Master of Arts (M.A.) program offers specializations in subjects like English, History, and Political Science. The first year focuses on establishing strong theoretical frameworks and research methodologies. The second year allows students to dive deep into specialized electives and culminates in an independent research dissertation.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Core Subjects: Advanced Theoretical Frameworks, History of Ideas, Research Methodology in Humanities, Academic Writing & Ethics. Total Credits: 20

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Core Subjects: Contemporary Literary/Historical Trends, Core Specialization Subject I, Core Specialization Subject II, Seminar Presentation. Total Credits: 20

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Advanced Studies: Elective Specialization I, Elective Specialization II, Interdisciplinary Studies, Synopsis Submission for Dissertation. Total Credits: 20

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Research & Culmination: Master's Dissertation (Research, Writing, and Defense), Viva-Voce, Elective Specialization III. Total Credits: 20

msw.title: Master of Social Work (MSW)
msw.eligibility: Graduation in any discipline with minimum 50% marks
msw.description: A professional postgraduate degree focused on community development, human rights, corporate social responsibility (CSR), and social justice.
msw.metaTitle: Master of Social Work (MSW) | Career Point University
msw.metaDescription: Pursue a 2-year Master of Social Work (MSW) at CPU. Gain hands-on field experience, NGO management skills, and prepare for a career in social advocacy and CSR.

hero.description: Empower yourself to empower others. A highly practical programme integrating classroom theory with extensive on-ground fieldwork and NGO internships.

overview.subtitle: What is the MSW Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Social Work (MSW) is a professional degree that prepares individuals to work with vulnerable populations, communities, and corporate bodies. The course bridges the gap between sociological theory and grassroots reality through rigorous concurrent fieldwork.

Whether you want to drive policy changes, manage large-scale NGOs, work in hospital settings as a medical social worker, or lead CSR initiatives for multinational corporations, the MSW programme provides the exact skill set required.

scope.body: The scope of MSW is expanding rapidly with the mandate of CSR (Corporate Social Responsibility) in India. Graduates are actively recruited by top MNCs for their CSR wings, international organizations like WHO, UNICEF, and UNDP, as well as top national NGOs. Career roles include CSR Manager, Project Coordinator, Psychiatric Social Worker, Human Resource Officer, and Child Welfare Protection Officer. The degree also offers immense scope for independent social entrepreneurship.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in the social sector

curriculum.subtitle: A well-structured curriculum designed for social excellence
curriculum.introNote: The MSW programme is uniquely designed with a 50-50 weightage on academic theory and concurrent fieldwork.
curriculum.outroNote: Graduates leave with a massive portfolio of fieldwork reports, rural camp experience, and a month-long block placement.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The Master of Social Work (MSW) program combines classroom teaching with rigorous fieldwork. Students spend 2 days a week in the field working with NGOs or communities. The final semester includes a month-long 'Block Placement' where students work full-time in an organization of their choice.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: History and Philosophy of Social Work, Sociology for Social Workers, Human Growth and Development, Social Case Work, Concurrent Field Work I. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Social Group Work, Community Organization and Social Action, Social Work Research and Statistics, Social Welfare Administration, Concurrent Field Work II. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-3
accordionSections.content: Social Policy and Planning, Corporate Social Responsibility (CSR), Specialization Paper I (e.g., Medical & Psychiatric Social Work / HR Management), Concurrent Field Work III, Rural Camp. Total Credits: 22

accordionSections.title: Semester-4
accordionSections.content: Specialization Paper II, NGO Management and Project Formulation, Research Dissertation, Block Placement (1 Month full-time internship). Total Credits: 22

applySteps.subtitle: Simple 5-step process to join the MSW programme

items.a: Candidates must have a Bachelor's degree (B.A., B.Sc., B.Com, BBA, B.Tech, etc.) from a recognized university with a minimum of 50% marks. Students from any stream can apply.
items.q: What is the eligibility criteria for MSW admission?

items.a: Yes! Fieldwork is the backbone of the MSW degree. Students must complete 2 days of 'Concurrent Field Work' every week in an assigned NGO or community setting, along with a continuous 'Block Placement' at the end of the course.
items.q: Is fieldwork compulsory in MSW?

items.a: We offer three major specializations in the 2nd year: 1. Human Resource Management (HRM) & Industrial Relations. 2. Medical and Psychiatric Social Work. 3. Urban & Rural Community Development.
items.q: What specializations are available in MSW at CPU?

items.a: MSW graduates have excellent placements. They are hired as CSR Executives in MNCs, HR Managers, Medical Social Workers in corporate hospitals, Project Coordinators in international organizations like UNICEF, and Child Development Officers.
items.q: What kind of career options and placements can I expect?

items.a: Yes, MSW graduates who choose the HR specialization are fully eligible and highly preferred for HR roles in the corporate sector, as they possess strong interpersonal and employee welfare skills.
items.q: Can I work in Human Resources (HR) after completing MSW?

items.a: It is a mandatory 7 to 10-day immersive field trip where students stay in a rural setting. They conduct surveys, study rural dynamics, run awareness campaigns, and learn community mobilization techniques hands-on.
items.q: What is the 'Rural Camp' mentioned in the curriculum?

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about the MSW programme

roadmap.subtitle: Your intensive journey to becoming a professional Social Worker.

years.contentDesc: The first year focuses on building theoretical knowledge of sociology and psychology, combined with immediate exposure to concurrent fieldwork in local NGOs.
Year two involves choosing a specialization (HR/Medical/Community), attending the rural camp, completing a research dissertation, and undergoing a month-long block placement.

m-lib.title: Master of Library Science (M.Lib.)
m-lib.eligibility: B.Lib. degree from a recognized university with minimum 50% marks
m-lib.description: An advanced postgraduate programme designed to transform library professionals into information architects and digital collection managers.
m-lib.metaTitle: Master of Library Science (M.Lib.) | Career Point University
m-lib.metaDescription: Pursue a 1-year Master of Library Science (M.Lib.) degree at CPU. Specialize in advanced informatics, digital library systems, and archival research.

hero.description: Master the complexities of knowledge infrastructure. Focus on digital library architecture, advanced information retrieval, and scientometrics.

overview.subtitle: What is the M.Lib. Programme?
paragraphs: The Master of Library Science (M.Lib.) is a highly specialized postgraduate course that goes beyond basic cataloging. It dives into the sophisticated realm of Information Architecture, focusing on how large datasets and academic archives are structured, preserved, and retrieved in the digital age.

This programme is tailored for professionals aiming for leadership roles such as Chief Librarian, Documentation Officer, or Information Scientist in universities, national archives, and corporate intelligence sectors.

scope.body: As the volume of global data explodes, the role of a traditional librarian has evolved into an 'Information Scientist'. M.Lib. graduates are urgently required to manage massive institutional repositories, design information systems, and analyze publication metrics (Scientometrics). Career opportunities span across premier universities, R&D organizations (like ISRO, DRDO), media houses, national museums, and top-tier IT companies requiring knowledge management.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities in Information Architecture

curriculum.subtitle: A curriculum designed for digital knowledge leadership
curriculum.introNote: The M.Lib. syllabus is heavily tilted towards Information Technology, Systems Architecture, and Research Methodologies in Library Science.
curriculum.outroNote: The programme culminates in an independent research dissertation, making students eligible for Ph.D. admissions.

accordionSections.title: Course Structure Overview
accordionSections.content: The M.Lib. program at Career Point University is an intensive two-semester journey. Semester 1 focuses on building advanced information retrieval systems and understanding the economics of information. Semester 2 is highly research and tech-oriented, focusing on digital library creation and a mandatory master's dissertation.

accordionSections.title: Semester-1
accordionSections.content: Subjects covered: Information Systems and Programmes, Information Retrieval Systems, Information Economics, Information Technology Application (Practice). Total Credits: 20

accordionSections.title: Semester-2
accordionSections.content: Subjects covered: Information Technology (Theory & Advanced Systems), Research Methodology in LIS, Informetrics and Scientometrics, LIS Dissertation. Total Credits: 20

research-phd.title: Ph.D. in Arts & Humanities
research-phd.eligibility: Master's degree in relevant field with minimum 55% marks & Entrance/NET/JRF
research-phd.description: A rigorous, highest-level academic degree focused on independent, original research in fields like Literature, History, Political Science, and Sociology.
research-phd.metaTitle: Ph.D. in Arts & Humanities | Doctoral Research | CPU
research-phd.metaDescription: Pursue your Ph.D. in Arts & Humanities at Career Point University. World-class mentorship, extensive digital archives, and a vibrant research community.

hero.description: Contribute original knowledge to the world. A mentor-driven research programme for the most ambitious scholars aiming to shape global discourse.

overview.subtitle: What is the Ph.D. Programme?
paragraphs: The Doctor of Philosophy (Ph.D.) is the pinnacle of academic achievement in the Arts and Humanities. It is not just about studying existing knowledge, but about creating new, original contributions that advance human understanding.

Scholars work closely with expert faculty to investigate complex socio-cultural, historical, and literary phenomena, culminating in a substantial dissertation that must stand up to rigorous peer review.

scope.body: A Ph.D. is the ultimate qualification for a career in academia and high-level research. Graduates transition into roles such as University Professors, Post-Doctoral Fellows, Senior Policy Analysts for think tanks, and Directors of Research Institutes. Beyond academia, Ph.D. holders are highly valued in top-tier journalism, international NGOs (like UN, Amnesty), and governmental advisory boards for their unparalleled analytical and research capabilities.
scope.subtitle: Expanding opportunities at the highest academic level

curriculum.subtitle: A structured timeline for doctoral completion
curriculum.introNote: The Ph.D. journey begins with rigorous coursework to establish methodology, followed by years of dedicated, independent research.
curriculum.outroNote: The award of the degree requires successful publication in reputed journals and a final public defense of the thesis.

accordionSections.title: Ph.D. Milestones Overview
accordionSections.content: Unlike undergraduate or master's programs, a Ph.D. is not entirely credit-based across years. It is milestone-driven. The only structured classes occur in the initial 6-month Coursework phase. After passing the coursework, scholars move into the independent research phases guided by their supervisor.

accordionSections.title: Phase 1: Coursework (First 6 Months)
accordionSections.content: Subjects covered: Advanced Research Methodology, Computer Applications in Research, Review of Published Research, Research Ethics & Publication Guidelines.

accordionSections.title: Phase 2: Synopsis & Registration
accordionSections.content: Scholars must conduct an extensive literature review, formulate a research gap, and present a Research Proposal (Synopsis) before the Research Degree Committee (RDC) for formal registration.

accordionSections.title: Phase 3: Core Research & Publications
accordionSections.content: The longest phase involving primary data collection, fieldwork, archival visits, and data analysis. Scholars must publish at least two research papers in UGC-CARE/SCOPUS indexed journals.

accordionSections.title: Phase 4: Pre-Submission & Defense
accordionSections.content: Drafting the final thesis. Presenting a pre-submission seminar to the department. Finally, submitting the thesis and defending it in a public Viva-Voce examination against external examiners.

admissionFee.subtitle: Transparent fee structure for Doctoral Scholars

admissionCriteria.text: Admission through University Entrance Test & Interview (NET/JRF exempted from test).

notes: Scholars who have cleared UGC-JRF will receive their monthly fellowship directly from the UGC.
The University provides assistance in applying for various state and central government research grants.
Outstanding full-time scholars without NET/JRF may be considered for University Teaching Assistantships (stipend based).

applySteps.subtitle: Process to enroll in the Doctoral Programme

items.a: Candidates must have a Master's degree in a relevant subject with a minimum of 55% marks (50% for SC/ST/OBC categories) from a recognized university.
items.q: What is the basic eligibility for Ph.D.?

items.a: Candidates who have cleared UGC-NET or JRF are generally exempted from the University's written entrance test, but they must still appear for the personal interview round.
items.q: I have cleared UGC-NET. Do I need to give the entrance exam?

items.a: The minimum duration to submit the thesis is 3 years (including the 6-month coursework). The maximum duration is typically 6 years, as per UGC guidelines.
items.q: What is the minimum and maximum duration of the Ph.D.?

items.a: Yes, you can pursue a part-time Ph.D. while working, provided you submit a 'No Objection Certificate' (NOC) from your current employer and fulfill the mandatory offline coursework requirements.
items.q: Can I do a Ph.D. while doing a full-time job?

items.a: Yes, completing the 6-month Pre-Ph.D. Coursework and passing the subsequent examination is strictly mandatory for all scholars, regardless of their prior qualifications (including NET/M.Phil).
items.q: Is the Ph.D. Coursework mandatory?

items.a: Guides are allocated after the successful completion of the coursework. Allocation is based on the scholar's research interest matching with the specialization and available vacancy of the faculty members.
items.q: How is a Research Guide / Supervisor allotted?

faq.subtitle: Everything you need to know about Doctoral Research

roadmap.subtitle: The milestone-driven journey to becoming a Doctor of Philosophy.

years.contentDesc: The first phase requires passing the 6-month mandatory coursework and successfully defending your research synopsis to officially register your topic.
The longest phase dedicated to primary research, archival visits, fieldwork, and publishing papers in peer-reviewed journals.
The final phase where the completed thesis is submitted, evaluated by external examiners, and defended in a public Viva-Voce.

community.label: Community
description: The Arts & Humanities community thrives on diversity, encompassing a dynamic range of thinkers, writers, historians, and social scientists.

We focus on producing individuals with strong social ethics, a deep understanding of human culture, and the critical thinking skills required to address complex global challenges. Our community regularly engages in literary fests, mock parliaments, and socio-cultural outreach programs.
